issue_summary: Ensure MethodSelector never returns null for parameter types
javadoc_before:
/** * Create a {@code MethodSelector} for the supplied class name, method name, * and method parameter types. * *

The parameter types {@code String} is typically a comma-separated list * of atomic types, fully qualified class names, or array types; however, * the exact syntax depends on the underlying test engine. * * @param className the fully qualified name of the class in which the method * is declared, or a subclass thereof; never {@code null} or blank * @param methodName the name of the method to select; never {@code null} or blank * @param methodParameterTypes the method parameter types as a single string; never * {@code null} or blank * @see MethodSelector */
javadoc_after:
/** * Create a {@code MethodSelector} for the supplied class name, method name, * and method parameter types. * *

The parameter types {@code String} is typically a comma-separated list * of atomic types, fully qualified class names, or array types; however, * the exact syntax depends on the underlying test engine. * * @param className the fully qualified name of the class in which the method * is declared, or a subclass thereof; never {@code null} or blank * @param methodName the name of the method to select; never {@code null} or blank * @param methodParameterTypes the method parameter types as a single string; never * {@code null} though potentially an empty string if the method does not accept * arguments * @see MethodSelector */
